module.name: histogram
module.description: A histogram computed from raw numbers: paste values in the body, it bins them and draws the bars (bin edges labeled). For distributions of measured data.
module.aiSpec:
A HISTOGRAM built from RAW VALUES — paste the numbers, it does the binning and draws the distribution with counts above the bars and bin edges below. Block module: the body is a list of numbers separated by spaces, commas or newlines (sections split by `<!-- @ -->` are merged). Use @barchart when you already have category→value pairs and @chartjs for full-featured charts; @histogram is only for raw samples. Params: • bins — number of equal-width bins, integer 1–24; blank (default) = auto, roughly the square root of the sample count (clamped to 3–12). • min / max — force the lower/upper edge of the binned range; blank (default) = the data minimum/maximum. Values outside [min, max] are dropped. • color — bar colour; default var(--accent-color).
<!-- @histogram bins: 6 --> 4.1 4.5 4.4 5.0 5.1 5.3 5.2 6.0 6.1 5.9 7.2 8.0 <!-- @end -->

param.name: bins
param.label: Bins
param.desc: Number of equal-width bins; leave blank for auto (about the square root of the sample count).

param.name: min
param.label: Range min
param.desc: Lower edge of the binned range; leave blank to use the data minimum. Smaller values are dropped.

param.name: max
param.label: Range max
param.desc: Upper edge of the binned range; leave blank to use the data maximum. Larger values are dropped.

param.name: color
param.label: Bar color
param.desc: Fill and outline colour of the bars.

snippet.label: Histogram (from raw values)
snippet.description: Bins raw numbers and draws the distribution.
